ogTitle: Lake Macdonald vs Lake Mary: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake Macdonald and Lake Mary.
heading: Lake Macdonald vs Lake Mary — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake Macdonald, Queensland and Lake Mary, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lake Macdonald has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Lake Mary (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake Macdonald or Lake Mary safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake Macdonald has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Lake Mary scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake Macdonald scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Lake Mary scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake Macdonald or Lake Mary?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lake Macdonald has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.93% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
